subject: Re: LSJ weather
body:
can we raise it instead

From: "LVJET@aol.com" <LVJET@aol.com>
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Cc: JEEPROJECT@YAHOO.COM; kellens@earthlink.net
Sent: Wednesday, June 27, 2007 11:28:31 AM
Subject: LSJ weather


LSJ weather looks good, typical afternoon thunderstorm, high 88 degrees.
mostly partly cloudy and nice hot weather.
 
ps: Gulfstream 2B paint question for JE.
I had Larry Morrison inspect the G2 paint progress, the location of the "N" is interfering with a communication antenna on the tail, 
He recommends lowering number 12" so that antenna does not inter fear with number is this OK?
please advise.

subject: Re: LSJ weather
body:
couldn't  we hydro seed , if we know it's going to rain for three days? why dont they clean equiptment in the shed.. de=rust. etc.?


----- Original Message ----
From: "Miles Alexander, LSJ" <littlestjames@yahoo.com>
To: Larry Visoski <lvjet@aol.com>; Jeffery E Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 24, 2007 6:56:26 AM
Subject: LSJ weather


Greetings
 
We are now on about the third day of heavy rain created by a local system. There is a local flood watch in effect. Some staff have called in because of flooding and water issues around their houses etc.
 
I am bringing in the staff who have arrived to continue cleaning up interior areas which was mainly achieved yesterday. This should not take all day. If the rain lifts we will clean up outside areas. However, there are rumours of a possible arrival today so I will hold staff on LSJ in case we need to prepare for your visit.
 
Please advise 
 
Regards
 
Miles



Mr Miles Alexander 
Little St James Island, 6100 Red Hook Qrts B3, St Thomas, USVI, USA. 00802-1348
Phone : 340-5139855, Fax : 340-7758108, Email : littlestjames@yahoo.com
